objects.name: Banking Organization (TTX participant)
objects.description: Bank operating SWIFT-connected treasury payment workflows.

objects.name: SWIFT-connected payment workstation and gateway
objects.description: High-trust environment used to create, approve, and transmit SWIFT payment messages.

objects.name: Payment fraud intrusion crew
objects.description: Threat actor seeking fraudulent fund transfers through treasury payment infrastructure.

objects.name: Abuse SWIFT-connected workstation to submit fraudulent messages
objects.description: Adversary leverages compromised credentials and workstation access to create unauthorized payment messages.

objects.name: SWIFT workstation compromise and suspected fraudulent transfers
objects.description: Simulated compromise of treasury payment infrastructure leading to suspicious SWIFT traffic.

objects.content: Treasury operations finds multiple unusual cross-border payment messages queued from a SWIFT-connected workstation outside normal business patterns.
The bank must decide whether to disconnect SWIFT-connected systems immediately, how to verify queued messages, and how to coordinate recalls with counterpart institutions.

objects.name: Isolate high-trust payment endpoints
objects.description: Apply rapid containment and break-glass operations for SWIFT-connected workstations and gateways.

objects.name: Manual verification and recall of high-value payment messages
objects.description: Validate message legitimacy, suppress suspicious traffic, and coordinate urgent recalls through defined channels.

objects.abstract: Inject 3: suspicious messages released before full containment
objects.content: Further review shows that one suspicious payment message was released before the queue freeze and is now awaiting downstream processing by a correspondent 						institution. Another queued message was stopped in time. Fraud analysts believe there is still a narrow window to request emergency hold or recall actions if outreach occurs 		immediately.

The compromised workstation has been isolated, associated credentials have been revoked, and no additional suspicious messages are found in the queue. One 	recall request remains under review by a counterpart institution. Business leadership wants SWIFT-related operations restored quickly, but security advises that reconnecting high-trust payment assets before minimum assurance steps are completed may introduce further risk.

objects.name: TTX Scenario 7 - SWIFT workstation compromise
objects.description: Scenario tests fraud containment, high-trust asset isolation, and cross-bank coordination.
